$description: Bundle-size baseline for the jfb bench app. Measures dist/main.js after `pnpm build-prod` (Vite production + minify). Each phase row records uncompressed / gzip (level 9) / brotli (quality 11) bytes plus a short summary of what changed.

0-baseline.label: Phase 0 — current main
0-baseline.summary: Initial measurement before any size reductions. Captured from main.js after `pnpm build-prod`.

1.2.label: Tier 1.2 view-bag leak closed
1.2.summary: Compiler emits per-component __view factory referencing only destructured primitives. Runtime mount/each/branch/lazy/clientOnly/ssr/hmr source the bag from def.__view; createView fallback is test-mode-gated via import.meta.env.MODE so Vite dead-code-eliminates it from production builds. Closes the all-primitives import chain through view-helpers.js.

2.4.label: Tier 2.4 drop __update fast-path emission
2.4.summary: Compiler no longer emits __update; runtime always uses genericUpdate. ComponentDef.__update + the runtime dispatch + the compiler emitter (buildUpdateBody, ~270 lines) all deleted. Costs ~3% wall-time on a 200-binding sparse-update microbench.

3.4.label: Tier 3.4 + view-bag cache (FINAL)
3.4.summary: View bag memoised on ComponentInstance via getInstanceViewBag (render-context.ts). createView fallback referenced ONLY inside the helper (not via call-site callbacks) so MODE-fold tree-shaking works.

5.label: Tier 5 drop __handlers fast path
5.summary: Compiler stops emitting __handlers; runtime drops the per-variant dispatch + _handleMsg. Saved 1,423 bytes uncompressed (1.2 kB gz) BUT caused 23-89% perf regressions on keyed-each operations (swap +89%, select +87%, remove +37%, update-10th +23%) per `pnpm bench`. __handlers' `method` discriminator dispatched to specialized reconcile paths (reconcileItems/reconcileClear/reconcileRemove/reconcileChanged) that the generic reconcile() can't match. Reverted — bundle savings not worth the perf cost.

2.1.label: Tier 2.1 variable-length binding tuples
2.1.summary: Compiler emits 2/3/4/5-tuple binding shapes for elSplit and a 3-arg short form for __bind; runtime dispatches on tuple.length / typeof. CORRECT but unprofitable for jfb because the bench routes nearly all bindings through elTemplate (row factory) and only has a handful of elSplit/__bind sites — the dispatch-overhead bytes exceed the per-call savings here. Reverted; would help apps with many flat elSplit bindings outside the elTemplate row path.
